ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tweets" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet */,
  "author_id" INTEGER /* ID of the author who posted the tweet */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the tweet was posted */,
  "tweet_text" TEXT /* Text content of the tweet */,
  "retweet_count" INTEGER /* The number of times the tweet has been retweeted */,
  "like_count" INTEGER /* The number of likes the tweet has received */,
  "reply_count" INTEGER /* The number of replies to the tweet */,
  "quote_count" INTEGER /* The number of times the tweet has been quoted */,
  "source" TEXT /* The source of the tweet (e.g., web, mobile app) */,
  "language" TEXT /* The language of the tweet */,
  "geo_location" TEXT /* The geographical location where the tweet was posted (if available) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_author_id FOREIGN KEY ("author_id") REFERENCES authors ("author_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "authors" (
  "author_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each author */,
  "author_name" TEXT /* Full name of the author */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the author */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the author */,
  "followers_count" INTEGER /* The number of followers the author has */,
  "following_count" INTEGER /* The number of users the author is following */,
  "verified" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the author is verified */,
  "profile_image_url" TEXT /* URL to the author's profile image */,
  "bio" TEXT /* The author's bio or description */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* The date and time when the author's account was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("author_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tokens" (
  "token_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each token */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet the token belongs to */,
  "token" TEXT /* Tokenized word or phrase */,
  "token_position" INTEGER /* The position of the token in the tweet */,
  "token_length" INTEGER /* The length of the token */,
  "is_stopword" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the token is a stopword */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("token_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tokens_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "stopwords" (
  "stopword_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each stopword */,
  "stopword" TEXT /* Stopword to be removed from tweets */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stopword_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_stopwords" (
  "tweet_stopword_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet-stopword pair */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet the stopword was removed from */,
  "stopword_id" INTEGER /* ID of the stopword removed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_stopword_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweet_stopwords_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "stemmed_tokens" (
  "stemmed_token_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each stemmed token */,
  "token_id" INTEGER /* ID of the original token */,
  "stemmed_token" TEXT /* Stemmed token */,
  "stemming_algorithm" TEXT /* The algorithm used for stemming (e.g., Porter, Snowball) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stemmed_token_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_stemmed_tokens_token_id FOREIGN KEY ("token_id") REFERENCES tokens ("token_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "lemmatized_tokens" (
  "lemmatized_token_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each lemmatized token */,
  "token_id" INTEGER /* ID of the original token */,
  "lemmatized_token" TEXT /* Lemmatized token */,
  "lemmatization_algorithm" TEXT /* The algorithm used for lemmatization (e.g., WordNet, spaCy) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("lemmatized_token_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_lemmatized_tokens_token_id FOREIGN KEY ("token_id") REFERENCES tokens ("token_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sentiments" (
  "sentiment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sentiment analysis result */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet the sentiment analysis was performed on */,
  "sentiment" TEXT /* Sentiment label (e.g., positive, negative, neutral) */,
  "score" REAL /* Sentiment score (e.g., 0.8, 0.4, 0.1) */,
  "sentiment_algorithm" TEXT /* The algorithm used for sentiment analysis (e.g., VADER, TextBlob) */,
  "confidence_score" REAL /* The confidence score of the sentiment analysis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sentiment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sentiments_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "topics" (
  "topic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each topic modeling result */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet the topic modeling was performed on */,
  "topic" TEXT /* Topic label (e.g., renewable energy, finance, technology) */,
  "score" REAL /* Topic score (e.g., 0.8, 0.4, 0.1) */,
  "topic_algorithm" TEXT /* The algorithm used for topic modeling (e.g., LDA, NMF) */,
  "topic_confidence_score" REAL /* The confidence score of the topic modeling */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("topic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_topics_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst, viewer) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* The hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* The last login timestamp */,
  "status" TEXT /* The status of the user (e.g., active, inactive, suspended) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* The date and time when the user account was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who accessed the tweet */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet that was accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Timestamp when the tweet was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* The type of access (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "visualizations" (
  "visualization_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each visualization */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet the visualization is associated with */,
  "visualization_type" TEXT /* Type of visualization (e.g., bar chart, line chart, scatter plot) */,
  "data" TEXT /* Data used to generate the visualization */,
  "visualization_title" TEXT /* The title of the visualization */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* The date and time when the visualization was created */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* The ID of the user who created the visualization */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("visualization_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_visualizations_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_visualizations_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)
